session_hint: the Kubernetes API pod restart investigation
question: What did we conclude was causing the api-gateway pods to restart every few hours in Kubernetes?
turns: Our api-gateway pods on the production EKS cluster keep restarting. kubectl get pods shows 47 restarts over the past two days. The service degrades noticeably during each restart cycle.
Checked application logs right before the last crash: no errors, no panics, no unhandled exceptions. The process just vanishes without any log output.

Ran kubectl describe pod on a just-restarted instance. The Last State section shows reason: OOMKilled. The Deployment spec has no resources.limits.memory set, so the Kubernetes OOM killer evicts the container whenever the node comes under memory pressure.

Set resources.limits.memory: 512Mi and resources.requests.memory: 256Mi in the Deployment. Restarts stopped immediately. Root cause: no memory limit configured, causing OOMKilled eviction under node memory pressure.
